{{Quote|Who are you?|Drazin confronting [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]]|<ref name="Episode V" />}}
'''Drazin''' was an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] [[Corporal/Legends|corporal]] assigned to assigned to [[Gamma Squad (Imperial)|Gamma Squad]] during the [[Occupation of Bespin]].<ref name="Episode V" /> He later served in [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Igar/Legends|Igar's]] honor guard on [[Endor/Legends|Endor]].<ref name="topps" />

==Biography==
{{Quote|Oh my! Oh, I'm terribly sorry. I—I don't mean to intrude—no, no, no—please don't get up—NO!|C-3PO to Drazin shortly before the latter shoots the former|<ref name="Episode V" />}}
[[File:3POPwnd.jpg|thumb|left|180px|C-3PO gets blasted by Drazin.]]
Drazin was born on [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]], over the gas giant of [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]]. At some point, he chose to enlist in the [[Stormtrooper Corps/Legends|Stormtrooper Corps]] of the Galactic Empire, eventually achieving the rank of corporal.<ref name="CCG" />

Assigned to Gamma Squad, he accompanied [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] to Cloud City in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]] to capture [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] during the Occupation of Bespin.<ref name="Episode V" /> While there, he and some other troopers were assigned to help the city's [[Ugnaught/Legends|Ugnaught]] engineers prepare the [[Carbon-freezing chamber/Legends|carbon-freezing chamber]], with an Imperial [[Protocol droid/Legends|protcol droid]], [[E-3PO (Bespin)/Legends|E-3PO]], serving as a translator. Him and another trooper were sitting down and jokingly referred to E-3PO as "E-Gregious" due to the droid's attitude. Insulted, the droid left the room,<ref name="IG-88">{{StoryCite|story=Therefore I Am: The Tale of IG-88|book=Tales of the Bounty Hunters}}</ref> grabbing the attention of C-3PO, Luke's protocol droid, who happened to be passing by. C-3PO entered the room out of curiosity, only to be immediately confronted by Drazin, who pointed his weapon and asked who he was. The droid tried to apologize but Drazin rose from his seat and blasted him, in order to prevent the Empire's cover from being blown.<ref name="Episode V" />

In [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], Drazin became a member of [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Igar/Legends|Igar's]] personal honor guard, and with that, [[Tempest Force/Legends|Tempest Force]], an elite formation of [[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial Army]], [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]], and Stormtrooper Corps units that was tasked with guarding the [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star's]] [[Endor shield generator bunker|shield generator bunker]] on the forest moon of [[Endor/Legends|Endor]], Drazin shot [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] when the Rebel [[R2-series astromech droid/Legends|astromech]] tried to open the bunker door, and the trooper was then promptly shot down by [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]].<ref name="CCG" />

==Behind the scenes==
In the non-[[canon]] comic series ''[[Star Wars: Tag & Bink]]'', Drazin's appearance in [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''The Empire Strikes Back'']] in the back room is changed into [[Tag Greenly|Tag]] and [[Bink Otauna/Legends|Bink]] hiding and being discovered by the droid. They shot C-3PO as payback for him causing their capture years earlier on the [[Tantive IV/Legends|''Tantive IV'']].
